posts.title: How I Frame Every Shot 1:1
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version": "0.3.1", "atoms": [], "cards": [["html", {"html": "

Somewhere between the brief and the deadline lives the actual work. This is a dummy post generated to exercise the Swarnil theme \u2014 headings, lists, quotes and code all render below so you can judge the typography honestly.

The setup

Every good system starts with a constraint. Mine is simple: frame the color 1:1, crop out the noise.

  • Start with the audience, not the tool
  • Measure twice, publish once
  • Delete more than you add

The details

Here is a code block to test the mono styles:

const frame = (life) => life.filter(moment => moment.signal > moment.noise);
Life looks better when you frame the color 1:1 and crop out the noise.

A smaller heading

Almost done. If you can read this comfortably in both light and dark mode, the theme is doing its job.

What you'll learn

  • Set up and configure a production Ghost site
  • Design routes, collections and channels that scale
  • Build a Handlebars theme from a blank folder
  • Turn members and newsletters into a tiny business

Prerequisites

  • Basic HTML & CSS
  • A domain name you actually like
  • Two evenings a week of honest attention

{"version": "0.3.1", "atoms": [], "cards": [["html", {"html": "

Somewhere between the brief and the deadline lives the actual work. This is a dummy post generated to exercise the Swarnil theme \u2014 headings, lists, quotes and code all render below so you can judge the typography honestly.

The setup

Every good system starts with a constraint. Mine is simple: frame the color 1:1, crop out the noise.

  • Start with the audience, not the tool
  • Measure twice, publish once
  • Delete more than you add

The details

Here is a code block to test the mono styles:

const frame = (life) => life.filter(moment => moment.signal > moment.noise);
Life looks better when you frame the color 1:1 and crop out the noise.

A smaller heading

Almost done. If you can read this comfortably in both light and dark mode, the theme is doing its job.

"}]], "markups": [], "sections": [[10, 0], [1, "p", []]]}
